---
title: "Publications"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

My research focuses on computational and data-driven methods for bioprocess systems, including CFD-informed compartmental modeling, dynamic flux balance analysis, population balance modeling, and reduced-order simulation of large-scale bioreactors.

## Thesis

::: {.publication-list}

::: {.publication-box}
### [Coupling Metabolic and Hydrodynamic Compartmental Models for Bioreactor Simulations](https://uwspace.uwaterloo.ca/items/d35d24f9-ca07-43a6-a724-d5bb6ca2d3b6)

**Ittisak Promma**

Doctoral thesis, **University of Waterloo**, 2025

This thesis developed computationally efficient methods for coupling metabolic models with CFD-informed hydrodynamic compartmental models to simulate large-scale bioreactor dynamics.
:::

:::

## Journal publications

::: {.publication-list}

::: {.publication-box}
### [A coupled metabolic flux/compartmental hydrodynamic model for large-scale aerated bioreactors](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0098135424002242)

**Ittisak Promma**, Marc G. Aucoin, Nasser Mohieddin Abukhdeir, and Hector Budman

*Computers & Chemical Engineering*, 2024

This work developed a coupled metabolic flux and compartmental hydrodynamic model for large-scale aerated bioreactors, integrating CFD-informed compartmental modeling with dynamic flux balance analysis.
:::

::: {.publication-box}
### [Modeling heterogeneity in large-scale bioreactors using the method of moments with a truncated normal distribution](https://aiche.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1002/aic.18922)

**Ittisak Promma**, Marc G. Aucoin, Nasser Mohieddin Abukhdeir, and Hector Budman

*AIChE Journal*, 2025

This work introduced a computationally efficient method for modeling intracellular heterogeneity in large-scale bioreactors using the method of moments with a truncated normal distribution.
:::

::: {.publication-box}
### Probe location matters: oxygen gradient measurements and CFD-informed modeling challenge the well-mixed assumption in bench-scale bioreactors

**Ittisak Promma**, Danny Kang, Zahra Negahban, Valerie C. A. Ward, Marc G. Aucoin, Nasser Mohieddin Abukhdeir, and Hector Budman

*Manuscript under revision*

This manuscript examines oxygen gradients in bench-scale bioreactors and shows how probe location and hydrodynamic heterogeneity can affect the interpretation of dissolved oxygen measurements.
:::

:::


## Conference presentations

::: {.publication-list}

::: {.publication-box}
### Integration of a flux balance analysis metabolic model with a compartment model for simulations of large scale bioprocesses

**Ittisak Promma**, Hector Budman, Nasser Mohieddin Abukhdeir, Marc G. Aucoin, and Alexandru Vasile

**CSChE 2023 / Canadian Chemical Engineering Conference**  
Oral presentation · Systems and Control Division Symposium  
Calgary, Alberta, Canada · October 30, 2023

Presented in the session *Large-scale Systems Control and Optimization*.
:::

::: {.publication-box}
### Formulation of Compartmental Models for Large-Scale Bioreactor Systems through Scaling Analysis of Population Balance Equations

**Ittisak Promma**, Nasser Mohieddin Abukhdeir, Hector Budman, and Marc G. Aucoin

**CSChE 2024 / Canadian Chemical Engineering Conference**  
Oral presentation · Systems and Control  
Toronto, Ontario, Canada · October 8, 2024

Presented in the session *Modelling, Control, and Monitoring Applications*.
:::

::: {.publication-box}
### Integrated Metabolic and Compartment Model for Simulations of Large-Scale Multi-Impeller Aerated Bioreactors

**Nasser M. Abukhdeir, Marc G. Aucoin, Hector M. Budman, and Ittisak Promma**

**2023 AIChE Annual Meeting**  
Oral presentation · Orlando, Florida, USA · 2023

Presented in the session *Applied Math for Metabolic and Bioreactor Systems*.
:::

::: {.publication-box}
### Oxygen gradient measurements and CFD-informed modeling in bench-scale bioreactors

**Ittisak Promma**, Danny Kang, Zahra Negahban, Valerie C. A. Ward, Marc G. Aucoin, Nasser Mohieddin Abukhdeir, and Hector Budman

**AIChE Annual Meeting 2026**  
Oral presentation · Minneapolis, Minnesota, USA · November 2026

Upcoming presentation related to oxygen gradients, probe location, and CFD-informed modeling in bench-scale bioreactors.
:::

:::
